title: Create your first alert rule - query

blocks.content:
Welcome to the learning path that shows you how to start your first Grafana alert rule. The query is the foundation of every alert rule — it defines what data Grafana monitors.

In this path, you'll open the alert rule form, give your rule a clear name, and build the query that fetches the data you want to watch.

## Here's what to expect

When you complete this path, you'll be able to:

- Navigate to the alert rule creation form
- Write a clear, actionable alert rule name
- Build an alert query using the query builder
- Preview your query results before continuing

## Before you begin

Before you build an alert query, ensure you have:

- A Grafana Cloud account. To create one, refer to [Grafana Cloud](https://grafana.com/signup/cloud/connect-account).
- Editor or Admin permissions to create and manage alert rules.
- A data source with metrics or logs to alert on.

## Troubleshooting

If you get stuck, we've got your back! Where appropriate, troubleshooting information is just a click away.

## More to explore

This path is the first step in creating an alert rule. After you build your query, continue with the condition, labels and evaluation, and handling and save paths to complete your rule.
